Stay Wild." and demo business locations listed in Palo Alto, CA and Hudson, NY, neither of which has anything to do with the actual North Chittenden, VT farm. Separately, bairdfarm.com/pages/our-story is indexed under the title "Meet the Crew," while a different page, /pages/our-story-1, is the one actually titled "Our Story" — the real story content sits at the duplicate-looking address instead of the clean one. On top of that, job/volunteer information is indexed twice: once at the clean /pages/job-opportunities and again at a cryptic auto-generated /pages/standard-pagej9elk, both carrying the same hiring and volunteer details.

leads.email_subject: One of your pages still shows demo locations in California and New York
leads.email_body:
Hi Bonnie and Bob,

I was looking into Vermont maple farms and came across Baird Farm — over a hundred years of family maple-making, with you two running it since buying the place from Bob's father in 1979, is a real story. I checked how the site shows up on Google though, and one indexed page (bairdfarm.com/pages/page-1) is still running leftover theme-demo content — the placeholder tagline "Eat Pure. Stay Wild." and business locations listed in Palo Alto, CA and Hudson, NY, which obviously isn't your farm. I also noticed your actual Our Story content is sitting on a duplicate-looking URL (our-story-1) while the clean our-story address shows a different page, and your job/volunteer info is indexed twice on two different URLs.

I put together a page showing what I'd fix: https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-07-20-baird-farm-maple-syrup.html

More on how I work at https://organic-theory.vercel.app

No pressure at all, just thought a hundred years of real Vermont maple deserved a site that doesn't tell people you're in California.

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder
